name: Corn Cakes with Sun Dried Tomatoes/Goat Cheese
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the sun-dried tomatoes and boiling water in a bowl. Let stand for 30 minutes.
2. Drain the tomatoes, mince them, and set aside.
3. Cut whole kernels from the ears of corn to measure 1 cup. Place the corn in a vegetable steamer over boiling water, cover, and steam for 5 minutes or until tender.
4. Combine the minced tomatoes, cut corn, and garlic in a large bowl and toss well.
5. In a separate bowl, combine the yellow corn meal, whole wheat flour, olive oil, salt, freshly ground black pepper, crushed red pepper, chevre, baking powder, and egg whites. Stir until well mixed.
6. Pour the milk mixture (made by combining skim milk and garlic in an electric blender) into the dry ingredients and stir until just combined.
7. Let the batter stand for 15 minutes.
8. Heat a griddle or large skillet over medium heat. Spoon about 2 tablespoons of batter per corn cake onto the hot surface.
9. Cook the cakes for 2 1/2 minutes on each side, or until the tops are covered with bubbles and the edges are set.
10. Turn the cakes and cook an additional 2 1/2 minutes on the remaining side, or until golden brown.
WARNING: Ensure the heat is turned off when finished cooking to prevent burns.
